target: data/sections.json part2b SEC-2b.1 paragraph restructure + HOL-REDUNDANCY consolidation strategy
assessment: Part 2b has already been substantially improved since the curmudgeon cycle-1 review (2026-04-06). Sections 2b.1-2b.4 now contain extensive concrete WIN examples, the derives_from_dome finding is headline-prominent in 2b.4 (individually scrutinizing all 4 exceptions), and the cross-tabulation (43→now 47 quadruple-failure WINs) is in 2b.4. The remaining HOL-COMPLETENESS gap is structural: 2b.1 still leads with hardcoded monitoring and treats none as secondary, despite none now being the majority (41/70 = 58.6% vs 25/70 = 35.7%). For HOL-REDUNDANCY, the eclipse/Chapman argument is spread across 6+ sections with full re-derivations — this is the highest-priority consolidation target but requires cross-section patches.

changes.description: Restructure the What the code actually does paragraph to lead with none monitoring as the most shocking finding
changes.old_text_starts_with: What the code actually does. We examined monitor.py directly
changes.old_text_ends_with: Only {{CA_LIVE}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}} actually fetch live data from external sources.
changes.replacement_html: What the code actually does. We examined monitor.py directly at commit 8e4bf9b (V51.0, dated 2026-04-05). The script defines 39 monitoring domains — each a dictionary with predicted, observed, error_pct, tolerance_pct, and pass fields. We classified each domain and cross-referenced to the corresponding WIN. The results: {{CA_NONE}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}} reviewed WINs have no validation code at all — they appear in the WIN list but monitor.py contains no corresponding domain. Over half the dome's "confirmed predictions" are not monitored by any mechanism. Of the remainder, {{CA_HARDCODED}} use hardcoded validation — both the predicted value and the observed value are static within the code, so the check confirms that a fixed number equals itself. For example, the Schumann Frequency domain (WIN-002) reads: predicted: 7.83, observed: 7.83, error_pct: 0.0, pass: True. The SAA Decay Rate domain reads predicted: 77.0, observed: 77.0, pass: True. Even domains that compute a prediction from dome constants (e.g., the Tesla domain computes VA/(2*DISC_R) where both are locked parameters) compare against a static observed value (1/0.08484), so neither side varies between runs. Only {{CA_LIVE}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}} actually fetch live data from external sources.

changes.canonical_location: Section 4.2 (the dedicated eclipse test analysis in part4)
changes.rationale: Section 4.2 has the most complete treatment of the eclipse magnetic depression, including Chapman mechanism, straw-man analysis, Kp<2 escape clause, and GUA 2016 scaling. It is on a tab (INDEPENDENT TESTS) that readers interested in falsification will visit. Other locations should use brief summaries with cross-references.

Reduce the Chapman derivation to a brief summary and add explicit cross-reference: "The Chapman mechanism, detailed in Section 4.2, predicts 5-20 nT depression from ionospheric conductivity changes alone."

changes.maintenance_benefit: If Chapman range estimate is updated (e.g., from new literature), only Section 4.2 needs editing. Currently any such update requires finding and patching 6 locations.

anticipated_objections.objection: The 2b.1 restructure is cosmetic — the numbers are template variables that already show the correct values.
anticipated_objections.response: Correct — the numbers are right. But the PROSE STRUCTURE leads the reader to perceive hardcoded monitoring as the primary finding, with unmonitored WINs as an afterthought. Cognitive science is clear: information position affects perceived importance (primacy effect). Leading with the 58.6% unmonitored finding is not just cosmetic — it changes what the reader takes away from the section.

anticipated_objections.objection: The eclipse consolidation will make the review less accessible to tab-skipping readers who only visit one section.
anticipated_objections.response: The hub-and-spoke model explicitly preserves brief summaries at each spoke location. A reader encountering the eclipse argument in Section 3.5.7 or 6.2 still gets the key point (non-discriminating, Chapman predicts same range) — they just get it in 1-2 sentences instead of a full re-derivation. The cross-reference lets interested readers go deeper. This is the same pattern used throughout the review for other recurring themes.

kernel_of_truth: The curmudgeon HOL-REDUNDANCY review correctly notes that some redundancy serves the tabbed layout — readers who only visit one tab should encounter key arguments. The consolidation must preserve brief summaries at each mention point. The goal is to eliminate duplicated DERIVATIONS (which create maintenance burden and drift risk) while keeping duplicated CONCLUSIONS (which serve different reader paths).
